known_steps: Reach the Poe gate room with pot displacement access.
Set pot position and Link angle for the displacement push.
Use the small bubble/texture cues to line up side-specific positioning.
Perform the pot displacement to get partial wall entry.
Adjust into the best angle for the final push.
Clawshot the rupee pickup to complete the clip through the Poe gate.
Input during animations that temporarily ignore stick influence (for example out of [Epona Slide](/posts/epona-slide)).
Buffer through the item wheel while moving, then hold `L`+ESS as it closes.
Stand by the gate-side wall where the displacement setup is done.
Position a pot for the displacement push (standard displacement setup).
Align Link so the wall push places Link partially into collision.
Perform the displacement push to get partial wall entry.
While displaced, clawshot the nearby rupee pickup to force the final clip through.
Move into the next room and continue route progression.
Find a clawshot target that is an actor (not static room geometry).
Position near a ledge/step where Link can enter a fall state without ledge grab.
Ready a method to stop displacement if needed (for example, bomb damage).
Clawshot the actor target.
As the target is being pulled toward Link, enter falling state by [Clawshot L-Slide](/posts/clawshot-l-slide) off a non-grabbable ledge (or very small step).
The actor continues moving toward Link until interrupted, commonly by taking damage.
For precision, use bomb damage timing to stop the actor along its path.
Stand in a place where Clawshot movement has a route purpose (for example, Lakebed clips or Cave of Ordeals movement).
Hold Clawshot to enter aiming state.
Be ready to input quick `L` taps with tight timing.
Press `L`.
Immediately release and press `L` again.
If timed correctly, Link can keep moving with Clawshot out instead of locking into normal stationary aim behavior.
Throw the boomerang at a carryable object.
While the boomerang is returning, trigger a short cutscene state (for example, a bottle-drink animation or other item/cutscene interrupt).
If timed correctly, the object drops where it was at cutscene start.
Need to have Master Sword
Need to have Clawshot
Need to have either Boomerang or Spinner
Need to have Lantern (or various less-straightforward alternatives)
Need to have Ending Blow
**Chandelier room:** instead of using the Double Clawshots, there exists a precise position in which you can skip clawshotting one of the chandeliers
**Tower climb Double Claws section:** you can clear this section with just the Clawshot
**Tower climb Spinner section:** you can clear this section with a combination of boomerang LJAs and damage boosts

**Beast Ganon:** instead of using the Bow or Ball and Chain, you can simply transform wolf to knock Beast Ganon over all three times. You don't even need to have Midna on your back for this to be possible
Enter gorge setup with BiTE state active (see [Back in Time](/posts/back-in-time) and [Back in Time Save](/posts/back-in-time-save)).
Use Epona positioning/movement to clear the gorge-side fence/gap segment.
Continue route progression on Kakariko side.
Begin pulling out Fishing Rod.

Interrupt the action with another state change (examples from the source page: mounting Epona during [North Faron - Early Forest Temple](/posts/north-faron-early-forest-temple-epona-clip), boar mount transitions, vines, ladders).
If successful, the duplicated rod remains and memory usage increases.
Perform [Back in Time Save](/posts/back-in-time-save).
Perform [Back in Time Equipped](/posts/back-in-time-equipped) with a valid BiTE save that loads King Bulblin 1.
Use reset timing during cutscene skip/void timing to freeze a boss flag value above 0, ideally `7+`.
Load into Forest Temple and route directly to the outdoor monkey swing progression.
[Epona Slide](/posts/epona-slide) gorge cross
Epona bomb boost over fence into LJA
Epona backhop into fence slide into LJA
Pick up an overhead object (commonly a box).
Stand close to a ledge over water.
Trigger a short text/cutscene state while still holding the object, usually by:
talking to Midna, or
using a bottle with contents.
If positioned correctly, Link drops the held object, gets pushed off the ledge, and falls while text is active.
Pick up an overhead object.
Stand in front of an NPC with a ledge immediately behind Link.
Talk to the NPC so Link drops the object and gets pushed off the ledge during textbox state.
Use a rupee value that still triggers its textbox on the current file (typically greater than 1).
Manipulate the rupee to the edge of a platform (often with boomerang/object interactions).
Collect it with a jump attack or spin attack so Link is falling off the ledge as the rupee textbox triggers.
Enter Kakariko bomb house sequence in wolf.
Position for the cutscene-delay setup and collect/kill the basement bug early (related consistency work: [Kakariko Village - Basement Bug Manip](/posts/kakariko-village-basement-bug-manip)).
Keep movement and camera stable during the trigger handoff to avoid re-activating the normal sequence.
If done correctly, bomb house progression is skipped/despawned.
Direct entry route using [Lake Hylia - Empty Lake Hylia](/posts/lake-hylia-empty-lake-hylia).
Requires [Early Master Sword](/posts/early-master-sword).
Modern route clips through the pillar and uses tap swimming timing to barely reach the loading zone.
Older setupless variants exist but are generally outdated.
Swim positioning route between the large blue pillar and adjacent green pillar.
Use upward swim acceleration and pass through collision into the loading zone.
Cue points: line up the minimap arrow northwest, remove Iron Boots, then hold target and mash swim upward.
Uses rupee text timing and a pre-input sword action to trigger dive behavior.
Historically useful, but mostly replaced by stronger setups.
Platform Midna Dive in twilight (version-limited).
Isle of Riches Midna Dive with Zora Armor.
[Bug Boss Midna Dive - Early Lakebed Temple](/posts/bug-boss-midna-dive-early-lakebed-temple) for specific bug-despawn timing.
Uses Ooccoo near plank edges to dive under floor collision.
Story window between finishing Lanayru Twilight and beating Lakebed Temple.
Iza river rocks must still be unbombed (Iza minigame not started).
A death during Plumm's minigame.
Enter Plumm's minigame during the valid story window.
Force a death during the minigame.
Respawn in Lake Hylia with state carryover, resulting in an empty lake state.
Nonzero boss flag state from [Back in Time Equipped](/posts/back-in-time-equipped) or compatible [Map Glitch (Warp Method)](/posts/map-glitch-warp-method) routing.
Entry to the Deku Toad loading zone while that nonzero flag is still active.
Build an Early Boss Fights-compatible state with boss flag value above `0`.
Route into Lakebed Temple and reach Deku Toad's loading zone before normal room progression.
Enter the loading zone to start Deku Toad early.
Commonly paired with [Early Master Sword](/posts/early-master-sword) progression.

Route into early Lakebed access (often via [Lake Hylia - Early Lakebed Temple](/posts/lake-hylia-early-lakebed-temple-various-methods) and/or [Lake Hylia - Empty Lake Hylia](/posts/lake-hylia-empty-lake-hylia)).
Clear Lakebed Temple and defeat Morpheel before normal Lanayru-twilight completion order.
Collect Fishing Rod, complete "fixed" [Early Master Sword](/posts/early-master-sword), and clear Goron Mines for Barnes' Bomb Bag.
Empty the bomb bag and catch Water Bombs at Eldin Bridge.
In Lanayru Twilight, collect all tears except the last one (after defeating the giant bug).
Beat Lakebed Temple before finishing Lanayru Twilight.
Pick a target you can lock onto (enemy, object, or Gale Boomerang pathing target).
Stand at your launch point and pre-align camera/facing for where you need to land.
Ensure the target is moving or positioned over higher ground/void relative to Link.
Throw Gale Boomerang over high ground or qualifying void.
Pull sword with `B` while preserving lock behavior.
Time the jump attack when the boomerang is in the favorable position.
Press `A` to release jump attack and carry the boosted speed.
Lure and target an enemy near your launch point.
Hit the enemy so it pops upward.
Jump attack while it is airborne to inherit higher-speed targeting.
The yellow rupee (or backup blue rupee) textbox must not have been seen in the current play session.
Link needs at least 1.25 hearts so the fence contact does not fail due to low-health behavior.
This is typically performed during [Gorge Skip](/posts/gorge-skip), but can be practiced standalone.
Align at the left fence-side setup spot used in [Gorge Skip](/posts/gorge-skip).
Throw boomerang using the documented target line so Link's roll path catches the fence edge correctly.
Roll at the fence to force the over-fence movement and grab/land on the outside ledge.
Continue with [Long Jump Attack (LJA)](/posts/long-jump-attack-lja) to finish crossing.
Trigger the post-messenger cutscene state at Eldin Gorge.
As control returns, jump attack into the gorge during the one-frame pre-warp window.
If the timing is correct, Link respawns outside Midna's forced warp trigger.
Continue with [Lower Eldin Field Gorge - Rupee Roll](/posts/lower-eldin-field-gorge-rupee-roll) or another crossing method.
`GCN`: open map and call Midna on the same frame, then choose a warp destination.
`Wii`: use icon shortcuts plus same-frame Midna/map input timing, then choose a warp destination.
`GCN UMD`: use [Universal Map Delay (UMD)](/posts/universal-map-delay-umd) to defer map timing, then force an interrupt.
If successful, Midna or another action interrupts the warp and leaves Map Glitch active.
A normal bomb available.
A water entry where Link has at least one eligible input frame before fully entering swim state.
Fall or jump into water.
Pull a normal bomb shortly before water entry.
If timed on the valid frame, Link keeps a normal bomb underwater and can place it.
Lure Hugo toward the Faron twilight barrier wall (Ordon Spring side).
Target Hugo and push him forward until Link is just before Midna's forced-send-back trigger.
Nudge Hugo slightly away from the wall so Link faces the barrier center while still targeting.
Wait for Hugo's attack, then do backhop -> jump attack.
While Hugo is still airborne, jump attack again for the extending LJA over the trigger.
`Slide method`: delay second jump attack slightly when Hugo is farther from trigger.
`No-slide (stick)`: hold back through first landing to avoid slide before second jump attack.
`No-slide (mash)`: can work in specific first-hit spacings but is less generally consistent.
Master Sword and Midna warp access for Midna-call method.
Frame-perfect A/B alternation timing.
For map-open method: map access and map warping enabled.
Talk to Midna and select warp.
Start alternating A/B on the shown input frame.
Keep alternating to hold map delay.
Stop inputs when you want the map to appear.
Open map with D-pad.
Alternate A/B starting on the same frame as D-pad input.
Stop alternation to release map.
Approach a vine wall and climb it.
Drop from the vines, face away from the wall, and place a bomb.
Turn back quickly and grab the vines.
After the fifth bomb flash, hold left or right and climb twice.
If timed correctly, Link clips through the vines.
On Wii, use Clawshot from an angle that takes advantage of wider practical aim angles.
Fire/position to force Link through the vine collision without bomb damage.
A completed Infinite Bomb Arrows setup.
A valid [Back in Time Save](/posts/back-in-time-save) flow.
For bomb transfer consistency, this works best when all three bomb bags are owned and filled with the same bomb type.
The Iza-side setup typically comes from [Steal Lent Bomb Bag / Black Rupee](/posts/zoras-river-steal-lent-bomb-bag-black-rupee), including the Wii-only Iza 1 escape variant.
Iza 2 availability (you already completed or skipped Iza 1 and can start Iza's second minigame).
At least 20 rupees to start the minigame attempt.
Access to Plumm's hawk minigame in Lake Hylia.
Start Plumm's minigame in Lake Hylia.
Perform a Kargarok out-of-bounds clip.
Crash Kargarok while out of bounds, then void and respawn on the OOB ledge.
For Infinite Bomb Arrows specifically, jump into the water as wolf and take the Iza retry prompt path.
After accepting retry and returning to Iza's hut, warp away; bombs/arrows remain infinite.
Lanayru Twilight completed and Iza's hut accessible.
Open portal at Upper Zora's River.
A setup method:
Wolf method: transformation access.
Ooccoo method: [Early Lakebed Temple - Various Methods](/posts/lake-hylia-early-lakebed-temple-various-methods) or any dungeon Ooccoo state.
Map methods: [Universal Map Delay (UMD)](/posts/universal-map-delay-umd) execution.
Wolf setup: force wolf state before agreeing to help Iza, clear shadow messengers, accept the minigame, then warp away after the wheel appears.
Ooccoo setup: accept Iza's bag, then use Ooccoo Jr. to return into a dungeon; bag stays in inventory.
Map methods: use map-open timing (including UMD variants) to warp out during Iza flow.
Wii bow variant: start Iza 1 and talk to Iza during the bomb-arrow rock cutscene timing to keep the lent bag.
